commands.review_summary:
This PR removes the OpenAI-provider predicate from `apply_patch` tool construction and updates one agent-tools test file to expect `apply_patch` for providerless, Anthropic OAuth, and subagent tool sets.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests 0. Total -6 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main gates `apply_patch` on `isOpenAIProvider(options?.modelProvider)`, so non-OpenAI providers cannot get the tool. I did not see live non-OpenAI after-fix proof in the PR body or supplied comments.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer approval of the broadened mutating-tool default before an automated repair or merge path is appropriate.

target.title: fix: remove isOpenAIProvider gate from applyPatchEnabled
target.body:
## Bug

 was unconditionally gated to only work with OpenAI providers (, ) via . This prevented the tool from being available on **kimi, anthropic, qwen, stepfun, and all other providers**.

## Root cause

In :


## Fix

Remove the  check. The  format is client-side (/ markers) and is not model-specific. The remaining  check still allows per-model allowlisting via .

## Changes
- Removed  function definition (no longer needed)
- Removed  from 

## Fixes
- Fixes #88357
- Closes #45269 (related — same underlying issue of apply_patch being treated as provider-specific)

## Verification
- [x] Tool is now created for all providers when 
- [x]  still filters by  if configured
- [x] No functional change for OpenAI providers
